description: Add group colors support - allow assigning a color to each task group for visual differentiation
steps: In src/App.jsx, update the taskGroups state structure. The default group should include a `color` field: `{ id: 'inbox', name: 'Inbox', sortOrder: 0, color: 'slate' }`. Define a GROUP_COLORS constant array with color options: blue, green, orange, purple, rose, teal, amber, indigo, pink, slate. Each color maps to Tailwind classes for: header background, badge background+text, and a left-border indicator.

In src/App.jsx, update the addTaskGroup function (line ~3134) to accept and store a color parameter. Default to a rotating color if none specified. Update the renameTaskGroup function to also accept color updates, or add a separate updateTaskGroupColor function.
In src/App.jsx, add migration logic for existing taskGroups that lack a `color` field - assign colors sequentially from the palette when loading from localStorage.
Export the GROUP_COLORS constant from a shared location (can be defined at the top of both TaskPanel.jsx and the new FullTaskManager.jsx, or in App.jsx and passed as prop).
The color will be used in FEAT-003 and FEAT-004 for rendering group headers, badges, and task row indicators.
acceptance_criteria: Task groups have a color field that persists across refresh
New groups are assigned a color automatically
Existing groups without color get assigned one during migration
The color is included in export/import data (already covered by taskGroups serialization)
verification: Run `npm run build` to confirm no compilation errors
